guideline: Emergency_Vehicle should be False unless the vehicle clearly performs emergency or law enforcement functions.
scenario: Apply Emergency-Vehicle attribute to the scenario below:
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Traffic light bulbs that are not illuminated and not visibly active in the front camera are out of scope.
scenario: A traffic signal housing contains several bulbs, but none are illuminated, and it is visible clearly from the left rear camera only.
title: What should the annotator do?

guideline: If pedestrian pose cannot be confidently determined due to poor visibility or occlusion, use Unknown instead of guessing.
title: Which Pedestrian_Pose attribute is most appropriate?
note: Answer independently for each of the 3 images below.

guideline: Vehicle attributes such as Brake_Lights and Turn_Signal should default to Unknown when visibility is ambiguous.
scenario: In a nighttime scene, only the side of a vehicle is visible, making the rear lights impossible to evaluate confidently.
title: What should the Turn_Signal attribute be set to?

guideline: Cuboid dimensions for the same pedestrian should remain generally consistent across frames.
scenario: An annotator resizes a pedestrian cuboid in every frame to tightly match changing walking motion.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Objects clearly visible in camera views should not be skipped even if LiDAR support is weak.
scenario: A bicycle rider is clearly visible in the front camera, but only sparse LiDAR points are present.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Objects not clearly visible in camera views or outside annotation scope should not receive cuboids.
scenario: A pole is barely detectable in LiDAR but cannot be identified confidently in any camera view.
title: What should the annotator do?

guideline: Unattended bicycles parked or locked without rider interaction should not be annotated.
title: Review the image below and choose the correct action:

guideline: Bicycles actively being ridden or walked by a person should be annotated.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Towed Objects must contain a valid Towed_by link to the towing object.
scenario: A trailer is labeled correctly, but no Towed_by relationship is assigned.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Objects visible only in LiDAR but not visible in any camera view should not be annotated.
scenario: A vehicle-shaped cluster of LiDAR points appears in the top view, but no corresponding object is visible in any camera.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Objects between 0-200m that are clearly visible in camera views should still be annotated even if LiDAR returns zero points.
scenario: A pedestrian is clearly visible at approximately 170m in the front camera, but no LiDAR points are returned.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Static object cuboids should remain stable across frames even if LiDAR density fluctuates.
scenario: The annotator changes the size of a parked vehicle cuboid frame-to-frame because LiDAR point density changes.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: Front camera visibility is mandatory for traffic light annotation.
scenario: A traffic light is clearly visible in LiDAR and side camera views but cannot be seen in the front camera.
title: What should the annotator do?

guideline: One traffic light system should generally receive one cuboid, even if multiple bulb housings exist. For 'Bulb_Count' attribute: enter total number of Traffic Light Bulbs, both lit and dark, contained in the Traffic Light Body.
title: What is the correct annotation approach?

guideline: If LiDAR points on a clearly visible object are systematically offset from the camera image across multiple frames, this is a calibration problem. Stop labeling, report to the project owner, and wait for explicit instruction before proceeding.
scenario: You notice point cloud returns are consistently shifted ~0.4m left of where vehicles appear in every camera frame.
title: Answer based on the guidelines.

guideline: If an object of a large category contains a smaller category object, label only the larger one. Exception: humans on the open bed of a vehicle (e.g. a construction worker in a truck bed) must be labeled individually. Do not separately label humans visible through vehicle windows.
title: What is the correct annotation approach?

options.b: 1 cuboid for vehicle, 3 individual cuboids for person

correct_answer_text: 1 cuboid for vehicle, 3 individual cuboids for person

guideline: If a region cannot be classified with high confidence and no class clearly fits, do not force a label. Do not use OtherObstacle as a default for ambiguous points: OtherObstacle is for identifiable hazards with no dedicated class. Flag and hold; do not submit with an uncertain label.
scenario: You see a cluster of points that could be fallen cargo or a large rock. You're not confident which class applies.
title: What should the annotator do?

guideline: Pedestrian_Pose attribute should be 'unknown' if a pedestrian appears to be in a state of transition, for example falling over, partially standing but not fully upright, or in an unusual yoga pose.
title: Which Pedestrian_Pose attribute is most appropriate?
In this scenario, the turn_signal attribute is marked as 'Left'. Is this correct?
